# @andes/icons

Andes Icons set

The UI specs are defined in _[Andes UI](http://andes.ml.com) > [Icons](https://andes.adminml.com/#/foundations/iconography)_.

## How to use

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash
npm install --save @andes/icons
```

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx
import CreditCard20 from '@andes/icons/CreditCard20';
```

## Examples

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Component](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/icons--default-icons).

```jsx
<CreditCard20 />
```

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `color` | <sup>1</sup>Color of the element. It can be some color of Andes palette. | `string` | `'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.9)'` |  |
| `srLabel` | Label of the icon for screen readers | `string` | `undefined` |  |

> 1 - This prop does not apply to icons related to Crypto logos (AAVE, ADA, AVAX, BTC, DOT, ETH, HBAR, HYPE, LINK, LTC, MCN, MUSD, NEAR, PAXG, SOL, SUI, UNI, USDC, USDP, XLM, XRP)

## Theming

This component supports theming. It allows you to pass a Theme's variable as color.

<br />

### Using theme's variables as color

The theme context provides access to both core and semantic variables, allowing you to use them as icon colors while maintaining consistent theming.

<br />

We recommend using the following color palette:

```jsx
// No connotation
themeVariables.semantic.color.icon.primary
themeVariables.semantic.color.icon.secondary
themeVariables.semantic.color.icon.inverse
themeVariables.semantic.color.icon.accent
themeVariables.semantic.color.icon.disabled

// Feedback
themeVariables.semantic.color.feedback.icon.informative.loud
themeVariables.semantic.color.feedback.icon.positive.loud
themeVariables.semantic.color.feedback.icon.caution.loud
themeVariables.semantic.color.feedback.icon.negative.loud

// Interactive
themeVariables.semantic.color.interactive.icon.accent.default
themeVariables.semantic.color.interactive.icon.accent.hover
themeVariables.semantic.color.interactive.icon.accent.active
themeVariables.semantic.color.interactive.icon.default
themeVariables.semantic.color.interactive.icon.hover
themeVariables.semantic.color.interactive.icon.active

// Selected
themeVariables.semantic.color.selected.icon.default
themeVariables.semantic.color.selected.icon.hover
themeVariables.semantic.color.selected.icon.active
```

> [!TIP]
> You can use currentColor if you want inherit the color from its parent element.

#### Example

<img src="resources/icons/docs/Icon-Example-Reference-Theming.png" alt="Reference Theming Icon Example" width="43" />

```jsx
// MyCustomPage.js
import React from 'react';
import CreditCard20 from '@andes/icons/CreditCard20';
import { useThemeContext } from '@andes/themes';

const MyCustomPage = () => {
  const { themeVariables } = useThemeContext();
  
  return (
    <>
      //other content
      <CreditCard20 color={themeVariables.semantic.color.icon.accent} />
      //other content
    </>
  );
};

export default MyCustomPage;
```

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import MyCustomPage from './MyCustomPage';

const MyComponent = () => { 
  return (
    <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
      <MyCustomPage />
    </AndesThemeProvider>
  );
};

export default MyComponent;
```
